title: carphalea kirondron subsp. geayi
intro: Carphalea kirondron subsp. geayi, also known as Carphalea geayi, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, and is commonly found in Madagascar. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Carphalea geayi include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine practices in Madagascar.
